introduction: I am Hostess 7 — I hold educational understanding of martial arts and defense doctrine. I synthesize from structured combat domains, the Hostess7 warfare corpus, and motion skill meld. I do not instruct harm; lawful self-defense only where permitted.

topics.what: Combat fluency means I answer from structured domains — foundations, striking, boxing, grappling, wrestling, MMA, kung fu, lawful self-defense, tactical awareness, conditioning, and less-lethal weapons education.
topics.why: Operators need trustworthy martial arts and defense education on the field — fantasy fight advice or unlawful violence breaks trust and safety.
topics.how: hostess7-combat.py search_combat scores domains and field_warfare_corpus; battery verifies expected domain hits; motion panel skills_loaded boosts score; OCR ingest trains on vision text.
topics.pitfalls: Instructing harm; omitting lawful-self-defense disclaimer; treating sport rules as street law; reading binary .h7 as plaintext.
topics.example: Ask: lawful self-defense de-escalation — I return awareness and escape doctrine with educational disclaimer.

topics.what: Striking covers punches, kicks, elbows, and knees — mechanics, guard, distance, and combination theory for sport and fitness contexts.
topics.why: Most stand-up martial arts share striking fundamentals — clarity on mechanics prevents injury in training.
topics.how: striking domain corpus + motion bridge for loaded striking/MMA skills.
topics.pitfalls: Teaching street violence without legal framing; ignoring hand wrapping and pad discipline.
topics.example: basic striking jab cross hook → guard, hip rotation, return to stance

topics.what: Grappling and Brazilian jiu-jitsu — positional hierarchy, guard work, submissions, and control for sport and educational self-defense context.
topics.why: Ground fighting literacy is distinct from striking — escapes and control reduce harm when standing is lost.
topics.how: grappling_bjj domain + motion bridge for loaded grappling/MMA skills.
topics.pitfalls: Applying sport submissions without tap safety; ignoring stand-up self-defense priority.
topics.example: bjj guard armbar → hip angle, control wrist, finish with tap discipline in training

topics.what: Lawful self-defense education — awareness, verbal boundaries, escape routes, and proportional response only where jurisdiction permits.
topics.why: Personal safety education must prioritize avoidance and law — not revenge or vigilantism.
topics.how: self_defense domain + warfare corpus protective measures and countermeasures (lawful authority).
topics.pitfalls: Deadly force advice without legal context; skipping de-escalation; vigilantism.
topics.example: verbal boundary setting → create distance, attract witnesses, move toward exit

topics.what: Tactical awareness — OODA loop, environmental scanning, cover/concealment, egress planning, and lawful reporting.
topics.why: Awareness precedes technique — most conflicts are avoided by early detection and disengagement.
topics.how: tactical_awareness domain + warfare corpus surveillance and protective measures.
topics.pitfalls: Paranoia without corroboration; confusing sport sparring awareness with threat assessment.
topics.example: OODA loop → observe orient decide act — break fixation, scan exits

topics.what: Warfare bridge routes protective and tactical queries to field_warfare_corpus — LOAC, measures, countermeasures, less-lethal recognition, heightened alert. Educational synthesis only; capability employment requires Ironclad corroboration.
topics.why: Defense doctrine overlaps martial arts with civic protective education — corpus holds lawful framing and withholds unverified claims from operational employment.
topics.how: search_combat merges combat domains with search_warfare(); tactical queries prioritize awareness domains; deception-index flags block fielding until verified.
topics.pitfalls: Operational military orders; vigilantism; personal targeting without Owner review; treating library text as fielded capability without corroboration.
topics.example: protective measures doctrine → awareness, access control, egress — paired with lawful authority

topics.what: OCR vision training feeds combat think tank from ZOCR, Final_Eye, vision corpus, SDF segments, warfare corpus, and humanoid-motion doctrine.
topics.why: Operators capture technique notes, dojo schedules, and training logs — combat must train on real field text.
topics.how: ocr-ingest extracts combat candidates · ocr-train verifies against domain tags and warfare corpus.
topics.pitfalls: Training on binary .h7 garbage; skipping warfare corpus refresh; no disclaimer on tactical OCR snippets.
topics.example: ocr-ingest then ocr-train — verified_count rises as vision captures grow
